tasks.name: GitHub repo scaffold + CI + deploy hook (`twlv20-ai-skills`)
tasks.description:
Outcome

The private repo twlv20-ai-skills is scaffolded with the per-tenant skill folder layout, baseline CI on every PR (lint + unit tests against the scaffold), and a deploy workflow that on merge to main SSHes to the droplet, pulls latest, and restarts the runtime via systemd. Branch protection is on. A no-op end-to-end deploy has fired successfully against the live droplet.

Repo layout

twlv20-ai-skills/
├── .github/
│   └── workflows/
│       ├── ci.yml              # lint + test on PR
│       └── deploy.yml          # deploy on merge to main
├── infra/
│   ├── schema.sql              # Plinko-authored, Keeper applies (Task 3)
│   ├── migrations/             # versioned forward migrations
│   └── tests/
│       └── rls_isolation.sql   # the RLS pairwise test
├── runtime/
│   ├── package.json
│   ├── src/
│   │   ├── index.ts            # OpenClaw entry; agent runtime bootstrap
│   │   ├── intake/             # Slack, WhatsApp, voice, Gmail intake
│   │   ├── integrations/       # ghl.ts, smartlead.ts, fbads.ts, vercel.ts
│   │   ├── runlog/             # Run-Log writer
│   │   └── tests/
│   └── tsconfig.json
├── skills/
│   ├── global/
│   │   ├── ghl-health-check/
│   │

tasks.name: Weekly encrypted Postgres snapshots → S3 / Backblaze B2
tasks.description:
Outcome

Every Sunday at 03:00 UTC, the droplet runs pg_dump over both twlv20 and infisical databases in custom format, encrypts each dump with GPG (symmetric, AES256), uploads to Backblaze B2 via rclone, retains the 8 most recent weekly snapshots, and emails on failure. A documented restore procedure has been exercised end-to-end on a scratch host.

Why this shape

pg_dump -Fc (custom format) — compressed by default, supports parallel restore via pg_restore -j, and lets you cherry-pick tables on restore. Plain SQL dumps work but can't parallelize and are 5–10× larger.
GPG symmetric (AES256), not asymmetric — one passphrase to manage, simpler operations. The passphrase lives in Infisical (Phase 1, post-Task-4) under backups.gpg.passphrase AND offline. We are guarding against B2 compromise or B2 lawful-access requests, not against an internal threat model where Infisical itself is compromised.
Backblaze B2 — cheaper than S3 for this access pattern, rclone supports it natively, and architecture §8 calls it out explicitly.
Weekly cadence matches contract §4 ("weekly snapshots"). For Phase 2 we add daily logical + WAL archiving for sub-hour RPO, but Phase 1 is weekly.

Pre-flight

Task

tasks.name: Infisical — self-hosted install + per-tenant scoping
tasks.description:
Outcome

Self-hosted Infisical runs on the droplet behind Caddy at infisical.<domain>, with one project per tenant + one global project, machine identities scoped per tenant, and the encryption key backed up offline. Phase 1 secrets that currently live in a password manager (DO password, app DB pw, API keys from List 01) get migrated in. The agent runtime fetches secrets at request time using a tenant-scoped machine identity.

Architectural notes (so Keeper isn't picking shape blindly)

Infisical needs its own Postgres database and Redis alongside the app. Reusing Twlv20's existing Postgres (Task 2) is acceptable for Phase 1 to avoid a second DB engine — create a separate infisical database in the same instance with its own owner role. Redis is new; install via apt.
Deploy via docker-compose using the official docker-compose.prod.yml from the Infisical repo as the starting point. Don't run via docker run directly — compose manages the dependency graph.
The encryption key in .env is the master key for at-rest secret encryption. Losing it = losing every secret. It does NOT live in Infisical itself (chicken-and-egg). It lives in /etc/infisical/.env (root-only readable on the droplet)

tasks.name: Tenant schema + RLS migration (per Plinko spec)
tasks.description:
Outcome

Plinko's infra/schema.sql migration is applied to the twlv20 database. Every tenant-scoped table carries tenant_id, has RLS enabled, has policies that key reads/writes on current_setting('app.tenant_id'), and FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY is set so the table owner is also subject to policies. Four tenant rows exist. An RLS isolation test passes for all four tenants.

How RLS will be used (so Keeper isn't guessing)

The agent runtime (twlv20_app role, NOBYPASSRLS) opens a Postgres connection per request and immediately runs SELECT set_config('app.tenant_id', '<tenant-uuid>', false); before any query. Every policy on every tenant-scoped table compares tenant_id::text to that GUC. If the runtime forgets to set the GUC, queries return zero rows (the GUC defaults to empty, no row matches) — fail-closed, not fail-open. Plinko owns the runtime side of this contract; Keeper's job is to make sure the SQL side is bulletproof.

Pre-flight

Task 2 (Postgres + pgvector) shipped.
Plinko has dropped infra/schema.sql into the twlv20-ai-skills repo (or attached to this ClickUp task as a file). If absent at the time Keeper picks this up, comment on the task and block until Plinko delivers.

Tabl

tasks.name: Postgres 16 + pgvector install
tasks.description:
Outcome

PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector running on the hardened droplet, listening on the Unix socket and 127.0.0.1 only, with a non-superuser application role and tuning matched to 16 GB RAM. This is the data spine the Run-Log, tenant schema, and Infisical all sit on.

Pre-flight

Task 1 (Droplet hardening) shipped.
DNS not relevant — Postgres never speaks publicly.

Step-by-step

1. Add the PGDG official apt repo (deb822 format)

Ubuntu 24.04 ships an older Postgres in its default archive. Use the PostgreSQL Global Development Group repo for current 16.x patches.

sudo apt -y install curl ca-certificates
sudo install -d /usr/share/postgresql-common/pgdg
sudo curl -o /usr/share/postgresql-common/pgdg/apt.postgresql.org.asc \
  --fail https://www.postgresql.org/media/keys/ACCC4CF8.asc

sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/pgdg.sources <<'EOF'
Types: deb
URIs: https://apt.postgresql.org/pub/repos/apt
Suites: noble-pgdg
Architectures: amd64
Components: main
Signed-By: /usr/share/postgresql-common/pgdg/apt.postgresql.org.asc
EOF

sudo apt update

2. Install Postgres 16 + pgvector

sudo apt -y install postgresql-16 postgresql-client-16 postgresql-16-pgvector
psql -V          # confirm 16.x
ls

tasks.name: Droplet hardening — Caddy/TLS, SSH-key-only, fail2ban
tasks.description:
Outcome

A DigitalOcean droplet (Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, 8 vCPU / 16 GB / NVMe) running Caddy as the public-facing reverse proxy with automatic TLS, with SSH locked to keys-only, root login disabled, fail2ban watching SSH + Caddy logs, and UFW configured. This is the substrate every other List 02 task installs onto.

Pre-flight

DigitalOcean account exists, Keeper is admin (List 01 → "DigitalOcean — droplet account").
Droplet provisioned: Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, 8 vCPU / 16 GB / NVMe, region chosen for latency.
DNS for the chosen hostname pointed at the droplet IP. Suggested subdomains: paperclip.<domain>, claw.<domain>, infisical.<domain> — all pointing at the same droplet, Caddy routes by Host header.
Local SSH keypair generated for keeper and jon (Ed25519: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C keeper@twlv20).

Step-by-step

1. First-login hygiene

ssh root@<droplet-ip>
apt update && apt -y full-upgrade && apt -y autoremove
apt -y install ufw fail2ban curl ca-certificates debian-keyring debian-archive-keyring apt-transport-https unattended-upgrades
dpkg-reconfigure -plow unattended-upgrades   # enable auto security updates
hostnamectl set-hostname twlv20-prod
timedatectl set-timezone UTC

2. Create non-r

lists.name: 03 — Channels & Foundation (Plinko)
lists.content: Paperclip + OpenClaw install, Slack/WhatsApp/voice/Gmail intake, four-tenant stubs, Run-Log emission, integrations. Owned by Plinko. Goal: end-to-end channel test by end of S2.

tasks.name: End-to-end channel test — Slack, WhatsApp, voice, Gmail all return drafts
tasks.description:
Spec. S2 demoable checkpoint (per sop.md §4 S2). Submit the same prompt from each channel; each produces a draft response within 60s; each row in runs is correctly attributed to its channel and tenant.

Acceptance criteria.

Slack /ai-task test passes.
Slack DM test passes.
WhatsApp inbound test passes.
Voice intake test passes.
Gmail intake test passes.
All five runs rows visible in Paperclip with the correct trigger_source.

Review gate. S2 demo with Jessie. This is the sprint checkpoint — if any channel fails, S2 is incomplete.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. All channel integrations + Run-Log + OpenClaw.

tasks.name: Run-Log — schema deployed, emission verified across every channel
tasks.description:
Spec. Confirm the runs table (deployed in List 02) is being written by every agent path. Schema fields: run_id, tenant_id, workflow, agent, timestamp, trigger_source, input_hash, confidence_score, HIL_status, outcome, token_cost, api_cost, duration_ms, artifact_refs, error_payload. Build a "Run-Log self-test" that triggers a run from each channel and asserts a row appears.

Acceptance criteria.

runs row written for every agent invocation (no path bypasses it).
token_cost and api_cost populated for paid model + API calls.
tenant_id correctly attributes to the originating tenant.
Self-test script in runtime/tests/run-log.spec.ts passes against dev DB.

Review gate. Jon runs the self-test; Jessie sees per-tenant cost lines in Paperclip.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. Tenant schema migration, OpenClaw install, every channel integration.

tasks.name: Vercel deploy integration — site publish path
tasks.description:
Spec. Wire Vercel API for the website build chain (List 04). Adapter exposes: create project from a GitHub repo branch, trigger deploy, read deploy status. Auth token in Infisical under the tenant who owns the site.

Acceptance criteria.

Vercel token in Infisical.
Adapter can create + deploy a test project from a branch and report URL.
Failed deploys surface in Paperclip + Slack.

Review gate. Keeper deploys a test landing page end-to-end via the adapter; Jon verifies.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. OpenClaw install, Vercel account (List 01).

Blocks. Website build chain (List 04).

tasks.name: Facebook Ads — read-only integration (SRU)
tasks.description:
Spec. Wire FB Marketing API in read-only mode. No write paths exist in Phase 1 (per contract §8 + risk §5.1 in path.md — account-shutdown risk). Adapter exposes: list campaigns, list ad sets, read insights for a date range. Token in Infisical under SRU's tenant project.

Acceptance criteria.

FB long-lived token in Infisical.
Token refresh job runs weekly; logs to Run-Log.
Adapter unit tests cover insights pulls.
No write methods exposed (compile-time guarantee or unit test asserting absence).

Review gate. Jon pulls SRU's last 7 days of insights via the adapter; Jessie spot-checks the numbers.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. OpenClaw install, tenant stubs (SRU).

Blocks. Weekly FB Ads report workflow (List 04).

tasks.name: Smart Lead integration — review/cleanup only (Phase 1 scope)
tasks.description:
Spec. Wire Smart Lead API in read + suggest mode. Phase 1 scope is review/cleanup, not running campaigns (per contract §8 / §10). Adapter exposes: list campaigns, list sequences, read sequence stats, post a suggestion to ClickUp.

Acceptance criteria.

Smart Lead API key in Infisical (global scope — Smart Lead is shared across tenants).
Adapter unit tests cover the read paths.
Write/send paths intentionally not implemented in Phase 1 — explicit comment in code citing §10.

Review gate. Jon runs an audit against an existing Smart Lead campaign; results post to ClickUp.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. OpenClaw install, Infisical install.

tasks.name: GHL integration — read + write (per tenant)
tasks.description:
Spec. Wire GoHighLevel API into OpenClaw with read+write for each client tenant. API keys per location stored in Infisical under the tenant's project. Build a thin adapter in runtime/integrations/ghl.ts exposing the verbs the Phase 1 workflows need: pipeline read, contact create/update, opportunity move, workflow status read.

Acceptance criteria.

Per-tenant GHL API key in Infisical.
Adapter unit tests pass against a sandbox / dev account.
OpenClaw can list pipeline stages for Pure Peptide, AgereSciences, and SRU on demand.
Write paths gated by HITL — agent never writes without approval row passing.

Review gate. Jon runs a live read against each tenant's GHL; Jessie approves a sandbox write.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. OpenClaw install, tenant stubs, Infisical install.

Blocks. GHL health-check workflow (List 04).

tasks.name: Stub four tenants in DB — Pure Peptide, AgereSciences, SRU, Twlv20 Internal
tasks.description:
Spec. Insert one row per tenant in tenants. Per architecture §6 each tenant carries its own users, credentials (Infisical project IDs), skills, KB, agents, runs, approvals, artifacts. Stub each with the minimum: tenant UUID, display name, Infisical project ID, Slack channel ID for tenant-scoped notifications.

Acceptance criteria.

4 rows in tenants.
Each tenant has a corresponding Infisical project (List 02 task).
Each tenant has a Slack channel (e.g. #tenant-pure-peptide) for tenant-scoped run notifications.
RLS isolation test re-run and passes against the real tenant UUIDs.

Review gate. Jon runs the RLS test from Postgres install task one more time against the live tenant rows.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. Tenant schema + RLS migration, Infisical install.

tasks.name: Gmail intake — ai@twlv20.com → OpenClaw
tasks.description:
Spec. Use Gmail's IMAP IDLE or push notifications to route inbound emails to ai@twlv20.com into OpenClaw intake. Sender allow-list initially: Jessie + 1–2 core team. Replies post back from ai@twlv20.com.

Acceptance criteria.

Inbound email → run row with trigger_source = 'gmail' within 60s.
Reply email goes back to the sender from ai@twlv20.com.
Unknown senders are queued for review (not silently dropped) and surface in Paperclip's approval queue.

Review gate. Jessie emails the address and gets a draft back.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. OpenClaw install, ai@twlv20.com setup (List 01).

tasks.name: Voice intake — Whisper → text → agent
tasks.description:
Spec. Provide a voice-intake endpoint: a Twilio voice number (or a recorder web app) that captures audio, hits OpenAI Whisper for transcription, and pipes the resulting text into OpenClaw intake.

Acceptance criteria.

Recording → transcription → run row with trigger_source = 'voice'.
Run-Log includes api_cost for the Whisper call.
Failed transcription returns an error event (not silence) and surfaces in Paperclip.

Review gate. Jessie calls the line / records a clip; the resulting draft appears in Slack within 60s.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. OpenClaw install, OpenAI account (List 01).

tasks.name: WhatsApp — Twilio inbound webhook → OpenClaw
tasks.description:
Spec. Configure the Twilio WhatsApp sender (provisioned in List 01) to POST inbound messages to https://claw.<twlv20-domain>/intake/whatsapp. OpenClaw maps the sender phone to a Twlv20 user, creates a run, replies via Twilio API.

Acceptance criteria.

Inbound WhatsApp message → run row with trigger_source = 'whatsapp'.
OpenClaw reply lands in WhatsApp within 30s.
Unknown sender numbers return a polite "this number isn't authorized" reply, not silence.

Review gate. Jessie sends a real WhatsApp message and gets a draft response back.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. OpenClaw install, Twilio account (List 01).

tasks.name: Slack — bot, /ai-task, DM intake, approval buttons, kill-switch
tasks.description:
Spec. Create Slack app under Twlv20's workspace. Bot scopes: chat:write, commands, im:history, im:write, users:read. Slash command /ai-task POSTs to OpenClaw intake. DM-the-bot routes to intake too. Approval messages render with two buttons (Approve / Reject); response posts back into the originating thread. Kill-switch command /ai-kill <run_id> halts a workflow.

Acceptance criteria.

/ai-task from Slack creates a Run-Log row with trigger_source = 'slack:slash'.
DM intake creates a row with trigger_source = 'slack:dm'.
Approval button click flips runs.HIL_status from pending → approved/rejected and triggers the next step.
/ai-kill <run_id> sets HIL_status = 'killed' and halts orchestration.
All four channel types are visible in Paperclip's filter.

Review gate. Jon walks through every command from Jessie's Slack account; Jessie watches a Loom of the same.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. OpenClaw install.

tasks.name: Install OpenClaw on droplet
tasks.description:
Spec. Self-host OpenClaw (MIT) on the droplet as the primary command surface. Configure to talk to local Postgres for runs and to Infisical for secrets. Behind Caddy at claw.<twlv20-domain>.

Acceptance criteria.

OpenClaw service running under systemd; restarts on failure.
Reads tenant + skill config from Postgres.
Reads secrets from Infisical at runtime; never from environment-baked vars.
One stub skill ("hello-world") executes end-to-end and emits a runs row.

Review gate. Jon triggers the hello-world skill from each connected channel (Slack, WhatsApp, voice, Gmail) and sees one Run-Log row per trigger.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. Paperclip install, Infisical install.

tasks.name: Install Paperclip on droplet
tasks.description:
Spec. Clone Paperclip.ing (Node.js + React, MIT) onto the droplet. Configure to use the local Postgres + pgvector. Deploy behind Caddy at paperclip.<twlv20-domain>. Wire to the Run-Log table for visibility. Reference: architecture §4.

Acceptance criteria.

Paperclip UI loads at paperclip.<twlv20-domain> over HTTPS.
Login uses ai@twlv20.com SSO or local accounts (decide per Paperclip config).
Paperclip reads from the runs table; an inserted Run-Log row appears in the dashboard within 5s.
Tenant filter selector works against the four stubbed tenants.

Review gate. Jessie logs in, sees an empty dashboard, can switch tenants. Jon inserts a synthetic runs row; it appears.

Owner. Jon (Plinko).

Depends on. Droplet hardening, Postgres + RLS migration.

lists.name: 04 — Revenue Workflows (Plinko + HITL)
lists.content: The four Phase 1 workflows: GHL health-check, weekly FB Ads report (SRU), website build chain, ClickUp infrastructure pass. Each has a skill folder, approval gate (Slack + Paperclip), kill-switch, and Run-Log emission. Demos in S3–S4.

tasks.name: Workflow 4 — ClickUp infrastructure pass (cleanup + per-client SOP.md + intake template)
tasks.description:
Spec. Per contract §8 / §9 / path.md §5.2. The first agent run on the live system. Cleans up Jessie's existing ClickUp (stale work, archive obvious dead tasks, normalize statuses). Establishes a __SOP.md__ doc per client (Pure Peptide, AgereSciences, SRU, Twlv20 Internal) defining how work moves through the system. Defines a project intake template every new build will use.

Acceptance criteria.

Skill committed under skills/global/clickup-infra-pass/.
Existing ClickUp audited; agent posts a "dead task" list for Jessie to bulk-archive (no auto-archive without approval).
Per-client __SOP.md__ shipped to ClickUp Doc per tenant, covering: how a request comes in, how the agent triages it, named human gates, where it ends up.
Project intake template pinned to each tenant's space.
HITL gates: every change to existing tasks routes through Jessie's approval queue.
Run-Log emits.

Review gate. S4 demo. Jessie reviews the cleanup proposals, signs off on the SOPs, accepts the intake template.

Owner. Jon (Plinko); Jessie approves.

Depends on. ClickUp integration (already live — this very ClickUp), OpenClaw, Run-Log, Slack approvals.

tasks.name: Workflow 3 — Website build chain (AI → Faguni → Keeper → Jessie)
tasks.description:
Spec. Per contract §7 / §8 / sop.md §4 S4. The HITL chain end-to-end on at least one site (real-estate site or 1220.com rebuild). Stages: AI drafts content → Jessie or client review → AI generates design → Faguni reviews → AI builds HTML/CSS or hands spec → Keeper builds/QAs → Jessie approves → GHL pipeline wired by Keeper → site goes live via Vercel.

Acceptance criteria.

Skill committed under skills/global/site-build-chain/ with stage-by-stage gating logic.
Each handoff creates a ClickUp task with full artifact context.
Faguni's review produces a markup/comments artifact in Paperclip.
Keeper's build produces a deployable repo branch.
GHL workflow tied to the form submissions on the live site (Keeper QAs the connection per §7).
One site shipped end-to-end during S4.
Run-Log emits at each stage; cost attributable per stage.

Review gate. S4 demo. Jessie watches the chain run; site goes live with him approving the final stage.

Owner. Jon (Plinko) orchestrates; Faguni reviews design; Keeper builds; Jessie approves.

Depends on. Vercel integration, GHL integration, every channel + Run-Log piece.

tasks.name: Workflow 2 — Weekly Facebook Ads report (SRU, Mondays)
tasks.description:
Spec. Per contract §8 / sop.md §4 S3. Every Monday 09:00 (Twlv20-tz), agent pulls SRU's prior-week FB Ads insights via the read-only adapter, fills a templated report, posts to Slack + emails Jessie. Recommendations included. Nothing is pushed to FB.

Acceptance criteria.

Skill committed under skills/sru/fb-weekly-report/.
Cron / Paperclip schedule fires Monday 09:00 SRU's tz.
Report template covers: spend, impressions, clicks, conv rate, CPL, top 3 ad sets, anomalies, recommendations.
Output rendered as a Paperclip artifact + Slack message + email PDF.
Run-Log emits with the API costs included.
First Monday delivery happens during S3.

Review gate. S3 demo with Jessie; first live Monday delivery is the acceptance event.

Owner. Jon (Plinko); Jessie reviews format and approves.

Depends on. FB Ads read-only integration, Run-Log, OpenClaw scheduling.

tasks.name: Workflow 1 — GHL health-check + cleanup (Pure Peptide, AgereSciences, SRU)
tasks.description:
Spec. Per contract §8 / sop.md §4 S3. Skill folder skills/global/ghl-health-check/. Agent reads the tenant's GHL pipeline, contacts, workflows, opportunities, calendars; produces a health-score report; queues fixes as ClickUp tasks (some routed to agents for auto-fix-with-approval, some to Keeper).

Acceptance criteria.

Skill committed under skills/global/ghl-health-check/ with: skill.yaml, prompt.md, tests/.
Approval gate in Slack + Paperclip — agent never writes to GHL without an approved row.
Kill-switch: /ai-kill <run_id> halts mid-audit.
Run-Log emits per audit with tenant_id, token_cost, api_cost.
Health-score schema documented (categories, weights, thresholds).
First three runs against Pure Peptide, AgereSciences, SRU produce useful, reviewed reports — Jessie signs off on at least one.
Each "fix" produces a ClickUp task with full context (links, artifacts, what was done, what's needed).

Review gate. S3 demo. Jessie picks one tenant, runs the audit live, reviews the report, approves a sample fix.

Owner. Jon (Plinko); Jessie reviews; Keeper executes any infra-side fixes.

Depends on. GHL integration, OpenClaw, Run-Log, Slack approvals.
